obligations.description: Catalog metadata update during migration (R30-R35) depends on catalog-replication (WD-06, not yet specified). Requirements R30-R35 must be revisited when catalog-replication is specified.

F02.R33 VIOLATED: readKeyIndexV2 does not validate blockIndex in [0, blockCount) or intraBlockOffset >= 0 at key-index read time. Corrupt values surface as IndexOutOfBoundsException (from CompressionMap.entry) or AssertionError/AIOOBE (from EntryCodec.decode assert), not the descriptive IOException R33 requires. Related to R40 PARTIAL.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F02 v2: code fix added bounds validation in readKeyIndexV2 + replaced assert with runtime check in EntryCodec.decode)

obligations.description: F02.R39g VIOLATED: when dictionaryMaxBufferBytes is exceeded, TrieSSTableWriter abandons dictionary training and falls back to plain-codec compression (dictBufferAbandoned=true) instead of failing with IOException as the spec requires. Either the writer should throw IOException or R39g should be amended to codify the graceful-fallback behavior.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F02 v2: R39g amended to codify graceful-fallback as the correct behavior)

obligations.description: F15.R27 VIOLATED: JsonParser uses mutual recursion (parseValue → parseObject/parseArray → parseValue) for nested structure traversal, despite the spec requiring "must not use recursion for depth traversal." The code comment at JsonParser.java:153-156 explicitly acknowledges the deviation, accepting bounded recursion (capped by R52 at maxDepth=4096) in exchange for simpler code. Resolution path: either rewrite as an explicit stack-based state machine in Materializer, or amend R27 to permit bounded mutual recursion with an explicit maxDepth cap.
obligations.resolved_by: spec-verify (F15 v3: Materializer rewritten to use explicit Deque<Frame> stack; parseObject/parseArray/currentDepth removed)

obligations.description: F10.R79-R84 VIOLATED (plus R5 PARTIAL): FullTextFieldIndex is a stub — all operations (onInsert, onUpdate, onDelete, lookup, supports, definition, close) throw UnsupportedOperationException. IndexRegistry.validate correctly enforces STRING/BoundedString field-type constraints for FULL_TEXT indices, so IndexDefinitions can be registered, but any table with a FULL_TEXT index fails on first write. Resolution requires wiring LsmFullTextIndex from jlsm-indexing through the jlsm-core interface boundary.

F10.R85-R90 VIOLATED (plus R6 PARTIAL): VectorFieldIndex is a stub — onInsert/onUpdate/onDelete are no-ops, supports() always returns false, lookup() throws UnsupportedOperationException. IndexRegistry.validate correctly enforces VectorType field-type constraints for VECTOR indices. Silent no-op mutations are especially hazardous because tables with VECTOR indices accept writes that are never actually indexed. Resolution requires wiring LsmVectorIndex (IvfFlat/Hnsw) from jlsm-vector through the jlsm-core interface boundary.

F41 (Encryption Lifecycle) has ~70 requirements covering per-field HKDF-SHA256 key derivation, KEK/DEK envelope hierarchy, key registry with atomic updates and CRC-32C, 4-byte DEK version tag on ciphertext, compaction-driven re-encryption, schema version tag in SSTable footer, leakageProfile() method, power-of-2 response padding, KEK rotation with dual-read window, and online encryption migration. Discovered during F03 spec-verify (2026-04-17): the only implementation is a single comment in DataTransformationAdversarialTest acknowledging GCM key truncation lacks HKDF. F41 was APPROVED after three adversarial review passes but never built. State demoted to DRAFT until implementation exists. Partial work landed under F03 Phase 5: MAC wrapping for OPE/DCPE (F41.R22 amendment), DCPE serializer integration, finally-block key zeroing. Full F41 build remains open. When implemented, the on-disk ciphertext format will need a migration pass to add the 4-byte DEK version tag prefix; existing data written under the interim format must be re-encrypted via the compaction-driven mechanism (F41.R25, R38).
obligations.blocked_by: multi-week implementation project spanning crypto, storage, engine, and manifest modules
